# Network Math Source Notes

Use these as the anchor sources for the network-math and verification modules.

## Coordination and manufactured attention

- Rogers, R., & Righetti, N. (2025). *Coordinated inauthentic behaviour on Facebook? A typology of manufactured attention.* Platforms & Society. https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/29768624251369784
  - Useful for: manufactured attention, timed link sharing, coordination as artificial audience fabrication, ambiguity between optimized marketing and inauthentic coordination.
- Yang et al. (2025). *Coordinated link sharing on Facebook.* Scientific Reports. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-00233-w
  - Useful for: statistical patterns in speed and frequency of sharing, moving beyond simple timing-only detection.
- Graham et al. (2024). *The coordination network toolkit: a framework for detecting and analysing coordinated behaviour on social media.* Journal of Computational Social Science. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s42001-024-00260-z
  - Useful for: multi-behaviour coordination networks, co-linking, co-retweeting, co-replying, labelled directed weighted multigraphs.
- Luceri et al. (2025). *Coordinated Inauthentic Behavior on TikTok: Challenges and Opportunities for Detection in a Video-First Ecosystem.* https://www.isi.edu/results/publications/20550/coordinated-inauthentic-behavior-on-tiktok-challenges-and-opportunities-for-detection-in-a-video-first-ecosystem
  - Useful for: synchronized posting, caption similarity, multimedia reuse, hashtag sequence overlap, short-form video-specific detection.

## Graph metrics and algorithms

- NetworkX documentation, centrality algorithms. https://networkx.org/documentation/networkx-3.4/reference/algorithms/centrality.html
  - Useful for: degree, betweenness, eigenvector, closeness and other centrality measures.
- Blondel et al. (2008). *Fast unfolding of communities in large networks.* Journal of Statistical Mechanics. https://doi.org/10.1088/1742-5468/2008/10/P10008
  - Useful for: Louvain community detection and modularity optimization.
- Newman & Girvan modularity literature. Use through standard network science references and current implementations.

## Bot and account-level features

- Yang et al. (2022). *Botometer 101: social bot practicum for computational social scientists.* https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9391657/
  - Useful for: feature families such as user profile, friends, network, temporal, content/language, and sentiment.
- Aljabri et al. (2023). *Machine learning-based social media bot detection: a comprehensive literature review.* https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s13278-022-01020-5
  - Useful for: bot feature categories, graph, temporal, behavioural, metadata, language, and network features.
- Bellutta & Carley (2023). *Investigating coordinated account creation using burst detection and network analysis.* https://journalofbigdata.springeropen.com/articles/10.1186/s40537-023-00695-7
  - Useful for: burst detection as early warning of influence activity.
- EPJ Data Science (2024). *Unsupervised detection of coordinated fake-follower campaigns on social media.* https://epjdatascience.springeropen.com/articles/10.1140/epjds/s13688-024-00499-6
  - Useful for: temporal activity similarity, shared content similarity, fake-follower campaign coordination.

## Diffusion and temporal modelling

- Hawkes process research for information propagation. https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fphy.2022.1019380/full
  - Useful for: burstiness, super-spreader effects, and self-exciting diffusion patterns.
